title: alchemilla australis
intro: Alchemilla australis, also known as New Zealand lady's mantle or Alpine lady's mantle, is a plant species in the family Rosaceae. It is a low-growing perennial herb with deeply lobed leaves and small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which have been found to have several health benefits, including reducing menstrual cramps and soothing digestive issues. Alchemilla australis can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used topically as a poultice or cream to treat skin irritations and wounds.
